title: Iced Galician Stilton Paella
description: A refreshing chilled Galician paella that marries the sharp tang of Stilton with the savory depth of seafood, finished with beurre-monte for silky richness.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a paella pan over medium heat.
Add garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Stir in the beurre-monte until it melts and coats the garlic.
Add the short-grain rice, stirring to toast the grains for 1 minute.
Pour in the white wine and let it reduce until almost evaporated, about 2 minutes.
Sprinkle the saffron and smoked paprika over the rice, mixing well.
Add the fish stock, bring to a gentle simmer, and cook for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Fold in the shrimp and mussels, cover the pan, and cook until the mussels open and the shrimp turn pink, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the crumbled stilton cheese until it melts into a creamy coating.
Finish with lemon juice and zest, taste for seasoning, chill the paella in a shallow bowl for 15 minutes, then serve over crushed ice.
